output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Get It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Send a legally-backed demand letter in 60 seconds. No sign-up. Just $19.99.
content_outline: Why Your Landlord Is Legally Required to Return Your Deposit
What Your Landlord Can (and Cannot) Deduct
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long Is Too Long?
The Power of a Formal Demand Letter
How to Send Your Letter in 60 Seconds Without a Lawyer
key_points: Landlords must return security deposits within a state-mandated timeline—usually 14 to 60 days
Normal wear and tear, faded paint, and minor carpet wear cannot be legally deducted
A professionally written demand letter is often the fastest way to force payment before small claims court
Deposits-Back.com generates and sends your letter in 60 seconds for a one-time $19.99 fee with zero subscriptions
output.cta: Stop waiting. Get your deposit back with a professional demand letter from Deposits-Back.com—just $19.99, ready in 60 seconds, no sign-up required.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters who have moved out and are frustrated because their landlord is withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit

tiktok_reels_hooks: Your landlord has 30 days to return your deposit. If they haven't, they're banking on you doing nothing. Don't.
POV: It's been 45 days since you moved out and your landlord just ghosted your texts about your $2,400 deposit.
That 'cleaning fee' your landlord deducted? Yeah, that's probably illegal. Here's why.
My landlord said I'd never see my deposit again. I sent one letter and got the full amount back 5 days later.
Stop asking your landlord for YOUR money. Demand it. 60 seconds. $19.99. No excuses.

short_form_scripts.hook: Your landlord is breaking the law right now.
short_form_scripts.script: If it's been more than 30 days and you don't have your security deposit back, they're not 'busy'—they're testing you. You don't need a lawyer. You need a demand letter that scares them into paying. Deposits-Back.com. 60 seconds. 19 bucks. No sign-up.

short_form_scripts.hook: I almost let my landlord steal $1,800.
short_form_scripts.script: They charged me for 'wall damage' that was literally a pinhole from a poster. That's normal wear and tear. Illegal. I sent a formal demand letter through Deposits-Back and got my full deposit back in a week. Don't let them bully you.

short_form_scripts.hook: 3 things your landlord CANNOT deduct from your deposit:
short_form_scripts.script: One: Faded paint. Two: Normal carpet wear. Three: Cleaning if you left it broom-swept. If they took your money for any of these, send a dispute letter immediately. Deposits-Back.com. 60 seconds. No subscription. Get your money back.
